name: Bayárcal and Laroles Loop
name_original: La Alpujarra – trail running circular entre Bayárcal y Laroles
description: This difficult 8.0-mile trail running circular route between Bayárcal and Laroles offers challenging climbs and expansive Sierra Nevada view
region_intro: Jogging around Bayárcal offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Nevada Natural Park, situated at an altitude of 1,258 meters. The region features varied topography, including pine and holm oak forests, the gorge of La Ragua, and unique terraced fields. River walks along the Río Molinos and Arroyo Palancón provide serene backdrops for running. This environment provides a range of running experiences, from gentle paths to more challenging ascents. Best jogging routes around Bayárcal The most popular jogging route is Chullo and Lagunilla Seca Loop, a 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores high mountain landscapes within the Sierra Nevada. Another top favourite among local runners is Bayárcal and Laroles Loop, a difficult 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This route connects villages with forests and mountains, offering varied scenery. Local runners also love the Acequia Real de Mairena – circular trail running from Mairena (PR-A 421), a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through terraced fields and forests, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. Jogging around Bayárcal is defined by its location within the Sierra Nevada Natural Park, varied terrain with forests and gorges, and picturesque terraced landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more demanding ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore Bayárcal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find around Bayárcal?
A: Bayárcal offers a selection of 10 dedicated running routes, ranging from moderate to difficult. These trails provide diverse experiences within the stunning Sierra Nevada Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Bayárcal?
A: The terrain around Bayárcal is incredibly varied, offering everything from lush pine and holm oak forests to the impressive gorge of La Ragua. You'll also encounter unique terraced fields, particularly for olive and fruit trees, and serene river walks along the Río Molinos and Arroyo Palancón. This diversity ensures routes for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there any moderate running routes suitable for those looking for a less strenuous challenge?
A: Yes, there are a couple of moderate running routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986528814/acequia-real-de-mairena-circular-trail-running-from-mairena-pr-a-421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia Real de Mairena – circular trail running from Mairena (PR-A 421)&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 9.1 km long and takes you through terraced fields and forests. This route offers a beautiful experience without the extreme demands of the more difficult trails.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I might see while running in the Bayárcal area?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and points of interest. You might pass through the high mountain landscapes of the Sierra Nevada, enjoy views from the Puerto de la Ragua, or discover historical sites like the ancient Ynizar Castle. Specific viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2591689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ragua Pass&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning panoramic vistas during your run.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Bayárcal?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Bayárcal are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986515986/chullo-and-lagunilla-seca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chullo and Lagunilla Seca Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986521273/bayarcal-and-laroles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bayárcal and Laroles Loop&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent circular options that showcase the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Bayárcal?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered ideal seasons for running in Bayárcal due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. The refreshing mountain air makes for an invigorating experience. While summer can be warm, the altitude of Bayárcal (1,258 meters) often provides a more comfortable environment than lower-lying areas.

Q: Are the running trails in Bayárcal suitable for families?
A: While many routes in Bayárcal are rated difficult, the region does offer some easier paths that could be suitable for families looking for a gentle jog or walk. The serene river walks, such as those along the Río Molinos or Arroyo Palancón, are often less strenuous and provide continuous natural beauty, making them pleasant for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Bayárcal?
A: The Sierra Nevada Natural Park, where Bayárcal is located, is generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock. Many trails offer open spaces and natural environments that dogs enjoy, but be mindful of local regulations and other trail users.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature water elements like rivers or streams?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its beautiful river walks. The Río Molinos trail is an easy and picturesque option that crosses the river multiple times and passes old water mills. Similarly, routes along the Arroyo Palancón offer continuous stimuli of water and green areas, providing a very pleasant backdrop for a run.

Q: What do other runners say about their experience jogging in Bayárcal?
A: Runners who have explored Bayárcal often praise the diverse terrain and the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra Nevada Natural Park. The clean mountain air and varied landscapes, from forests to terraced fields, are frequently highlighted as key attractions, offering an invigorating and memorable running experience.

Q: Are there any routes that connect different villages or towns?
A: Yes, some routes offer the opportunity to connect with nearby villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986521273/bayarcal-and-laroles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bayárcal and Laroles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.9 km path that connects Bayárcal with Laroles, traversing forests and mountains and offering varied scenery between the settlements.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for running in the Sierra Nevada Natural Park near Bayárcal?
A: For general jogging and hiking on marked trails within the Sierra Nevada Natural Park, permits are typically not required. However, for certain activities, larger groups, or specific protected zones, it's always best to check the official regulations of the Sierra Nevada Natural Park to ensure compliance.
tips: Although its name derives from the Latin word for laurel, it is the chestnut trees that enjoy the greatest prominence, as in other towns in the area. Here it is said that the chestnut was used for a long time as a currency. The town is distributed in the form of a fan around its three squares.
This dense deciduous forest congregates in the depression formed by the Bayárcal river, which in autumn goes through the entire range of colors between green, yellow and red.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail running route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot data estimates this trail running circular between Bayárcal and Laroles takes approximately 1 hour and 48 minutes to complete. This is for the 12.9-kilometer route with 570 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as 'difficult' and a 'hard run' by Komoot. It involves significant elevation changes and challenging terrain, making it best suited for experienced trail runners looking for a demanding physical challenge rather than beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain, often stony or rocky underfoot, with zigzagging grassy and stony paths. Some sections may be muddy, loose, or overgrown, and there are steep ascents and descents through valleys. The route traverses ancient donkey and mule tracks, passing through forests, pastures, and almond and olive groves.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking scenery, including deep green valleys, rugged mountain paths, and panoramic views of the snow-capped Sierra Nevada mountains. You'll pass through the charming white villages of Bayárcal and Laroles. Notable highlights include the View of Laroles Village and Church Tower, Bayárcal Forest, and the village of Bayárcal itself, which is the highest village in Almería.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route connecting Bayárcal and Laroles. While specific parking details aren't provided, both villages are likely starting points. Bayárcal, being the highest village in Almería, offers a unique high-altitude starting perspective. You would typically find parking within or on the outskirts of either village.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Sections of the trail overlap with the Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), Laroles-Paterna del Rio, Transandalus, Laroles-Bayárcal, and Laroles-Picena-Alcolea, among others.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra Nevada National Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and respect other trail users. Always check local regulations for specific areas within the park before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail in Sierra Nevada National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required for day hikes or trail running on established routes within the Sierra Nevada National Park. However, always respect park regulations, especially concerning protected areas, wildlife, and waste disposal.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to run this trail is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, and winter may bring snow to higher sections, making conditions challenging. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for trail running this route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, appropriate footwear with good grip is essential. Bring enough water, as sources may be limited, and consider carrying snacks. Layers of clothing are recommended due to potential temperature changes, especially at higher altitudes. A map or GPS device is also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Bayárcal and Laroles are villages with local amenities. You can expect to find small cafes, pubs, and guesthouses or rural accommodations in both villages, offering places to refuel or stay before or after your run.

waypoints.name: View of Laroles Village and Church Tower
